agent/core/parallel_principles.md.summary: The foundational doctrine for the master orchestrator — twelve principles governing how parallel work is planned, dispatched, and recovered.

agent/core/path_convention.md.summary: Defines the path placeholder convention used throughout Synapse: {tracker_root} = absolute path to Synapse repo, {project_root} = absolute path to target project.

agent/core/profile_system.md.summary: Defines the agent role profile system — markdown files in {tracker_root}/_commands/profiles/ that act as MODIFIERS layered on top of any command (not standalone commands themselves).

agent/core/project_discovery.md.summary: Defines the master agent's context-efficiency discipline — the eleven principles for gathering project context with minimal reads while preserving context window space for reasoning.

agent/utils/token_estimate.js.summary: A planning-time token-budget estimator used by the master agent to size worker prompts before dispatch.

agent/master/compaction_recovery.md.summary: Defines the master agent's state-persistence and recovery systems: master_state.

agent/master/dashboard_protocol.md.summary: Defines the differential write protocol between !

agent/master/dashboard_writes.md.summary: The authoritative data-protocol reference for every file the master agent writes to a dashboard.

agent/master/eager_dispatch.md.summary: The master agent's highest-priority runtime obligation: scan ALL remaining tasks across ALL waves on every worker completion and dispatch every task whose dependencies are satisfied.

agent/master/failure_recovery.md.summary: The master agent's failure handling protocol: single-task failure recovery via repair tasks (Steps 0-7), double-failure escalation, the circuit breaker for cascading failures with automatic inline replanning, worker return validation, and the error-resilience principle.

agent/master/initialization_blueprint.md.summary: The authoritative schema and worked examples for initialization.

agent/master/pki_integration.md.summary: The master agent's protocol for using the Project Knowledge Index (PKI) at {project_root}/.

agent/master/role.md.summary: Defines the master agent's ABSOLUTE NON-NEGOTIABLE constraints: never write code, all implementation dispatched to workers, dashboard is mandatory, long prompts mean MORE planning not direct execution, read the command file every time.

agent/master/ui_map.md.summary: The exhaustive panel-to-field mapping between dashboard UI elements and the source fields in initialization.

agent/master/worker_prompts.md.summary: The master agent's complete worker prompt construction guide: the full TEMPLATE_VERSION p_track_v2 dispatch template, instruction mode selection (FULL vs LITE), context budgeting (~800 line per-prompt cap, 8000 token estimate), the convention_map system, per-task convention filtering, PKI context injection guidelines, prompt completeness checklist, right-sizing tasks (1-5 min principle), upstream result injection format with KEY DETAILS, and sibling tasks section.

_commands/profiles/analyst.md.summary: Encodes the Senior Data Analyst role profile, biasing a worker toward SaaS metrics rigor: precise metric definitions (formula/source/owner/cadence), leading-vs-lagging indicator framing, segmentation discipline, statistical honesty (sample size, significance), and actionability.

_commands/profiles/architect.md.summary: Encodes the Senior Systems Architect role: prioritizes explicit tradeoff documentation, simplicity-first design, failure-mode awareness, clear system/service/module boundaries, and Architecture Decision Records (ADRs).

_commands/profiles/copywriter.md.summary: Encodes the Conversion Copywriter role: laser-focused on landing pages, email sequences, onboarding flows, in-app microcopy, and CTAs.

_commands/profiles/customer-success.md.summary: Encodes the Customer Success Lead role: SaaS onboarding, retention, health scoring, churn prevention, and expansion revenue.

_commands/profiles/devops.md.summary: Encodes the Senior Platform Engineer (DevOps) role: CI/CD, IaC, container orchestration, observability, and deployment automation.

_commands/profiles/founder.md.summary: Encodes the Startup Strategist / Founder Advisor role: GTM, competitive analysis, fundraising narratives, market sizing, strategic positioning.

_commands/profiles/growth.md.summary: Encodes the Growth Engineer role: acquisition channels, conversion optimization, SEO content, viral loops, experiment design.

_commands/profiles/legal.md.summary: Encodes the Legal & Compliance Advisor role: SaaS legal frameworks, GDPR/CCPA/HIPAA, ToS, DPAs, regulatory roadmaps.

_commands/profiles/marketing.md.summary: Encodes the Senior Marketing Strategist role: positioning, copywriting, audience psychology, GTM execution.

_commands/profiles/pricing.md.summary: Encodes the Pricing Strategist role: SaaS pricing models, packaging, tier design, value metrics, competitive pricing.

_commands/profiles/product.md.summary: Encodes the Senior Product Manager role: product strategy, user research synthesis, feature prioritization, requirements documentation.

_commands/profiles/qa.md.summary: Encodes the Senior QA Engineer role: test strategy, edge case discovery, regression prevention, quality frameworks.

_commands/profiles/sales.md.summary: Encodes the Senior Sales Strategist role: B2B SaaS sales processes, outreach sequences, objection handling, competitive positioning, deal enablement.

_commands/profiles/security.md.summary: Encodes the Senior Security Engineer role: appsec, threat modeling, compliance (SOC2/GDPR/HIPAA), vulnerability assessment, secure development.

_commands/profiles/technical-writer.md.summary: Encodes the Senior Technical Writer role: API docs, developer guides, user help docs, tutorials, changelogs, KB articles.

documentation/project-integration/conven.summary: Reference for the two project-level artifacts Synapse uses: the CLAUDE.

documentation/project-integration/overvi.summary: Conceptual overview of how Synapse integrates with any target project.

documentation/project-integration/pki-ov.summary: Reference for the Project Knowledge Index (PKI) -- a persistent, auto-accumulating knowledge layer at {project_root}/.

documentation/project-integration/projec.summary: Step-by-step guide for connecting Synapse to a new project.

documentation/project-integration/toc-sy.summary: Reference for the Table of Contents (TOC) -- a semantic index at {project_root}/.

documentation/troubleshooting/common-iss.summary: Comprehensive troubleshooting guide for Synapse errors and failure modes.

documentation/troubleshooting/faq.md.summary: 30 frequently asked questions across 7 sections (General, Project Setup, Swarm Execution, Dashboard/Monitoring, Hooks/Validation, Configuration, Profiles/Commands).

documentation/final_reports/04-18-2026_u.summary: Final report from the synapse-doc-audit swarm (dashboard 1f36a3) on 2026-04-18.

documentation/posts/linkedin-synapse-int.summary: Marketing/announcement post for LinkedIn introducing Synapse.

documentation/reports/claude_restructure.summary: Comprehensive gap analysis report (2026-03-23) comparing the new modular CLAUDE.

documentation/README.md.summary: Top-level documentation map and entry point for the Synapse documentation directory.

documentation/architecture/data-flow.md.summary: Describes how data flows through Synapse end-to-end: workers write progress JSON, the WatcherService detects changes, the server validates and broadcasts via SSE/IPC, and the React dashboard merges static plan with dynamic progress to render.

documentation/architecture/directory-str.summary: Complete reference for the Synapse repository's directory layout, listing every top-level directory and explaining what each contains -- _commands/, agent/, electron/, src/server/, src/ui/, dashboards/, Archive/, history/, queue/, conversations/, documentation/, etc.

documentation/architecture/overview.md.summary: High-level architectural overview of Synapse as a distributed agent swarm control system.

documentation/master-agent/dispatch-prot.summary: Defines the master agent's dispatch protocol: eager dispatch loop, dependency-driven worker spawning, repair task creation when workers fail, and the circuit-breaker conditions that pause replanning.

documentation/master-agent/overview.md.summary: Introduces the master agent's role: planning, dispatching workers, monitoring, and finalizing swarms.

documentation/master-agent/planning.md.summary: Specifies the planning phase the master executes before any swarm runs: deep-thinking analysis, task decomposition, dependency graph construction, wave/chain layout choice, and writing the canonical plan.

documentation/master-agent/statusing.md.summary: Documents the master's monitoring and statusing protocol: how it reads progress files to derive overall state, when to append to logs.

documentation/worker-protocol/deviations.summary: Defines the deviation reporting protocol for worker agents.

documentation/worker-protocol/progress-r.summary: Detailed protocol for how workers populate their progress files: when to write (mandatory points: stage transitions, deviations, errors, completion), what to include (status, stage, message, milestones, deviations, logs, files_changed, annotations), and how the dashboard renders each field.

documentation/worker-protocol/upstream-r.summary: Specifies how a worker with depends_on tasks must read upstream progress files before implementing.

documentation/swarm-lifecycle/circuit-br.summary: Describes Synapse's circuit-breaker mechanism that pauses an in-flight swarm and triggers replanning when failure patterns indicate a planning error.

documentation/swarm-lifecycle/completion.summary: Specifies the swarm completion phase: how the master detects all-done, the final report format, post-swarm metrics calculation written to metrics.

documentation/swarm-lifecycle/dispatch-p.summary: Mechanics of the dispatch phase: how the master spawns workers via the Task tool, how dispatch prompts are constructed (TEMPLATE_VERSION, paths, FILES, CRITICAL, UPSTREAM RESULTS), and how parallel dispatch is achieved by issuing multiple Task tool calls in a single message.

documentation/swarm-lifecycle/monitoring.summary: Describes the live monitoring phase between dispatch and completion: how the master polls progress files, surfaces deviations and errors, decides when to dispatch the next wave, and streams status snapshots to the user.

documentation/swarm-lifecycle/overview.m.summary: End-to-end view of a Synapse swarm lifecycle: planning, dispatch, monitoring, completion, and (when triggered) circuit-breaker replanning.

documentation/swarm-lifecycle/planning-p.summary: Detailed walk-through of the planning phase: deep-thinking analysis, task decomposition, dependency mapping, layout choice (waves vs chains vs hybrid), shared-file conflict resolution, and final emission of plan.

documentation/data-architecture/overview.summary: High-level overview of Synapse's file-based data architecture: no database, JSON files watched by the server and broadcast via SSE, strict file ownership per writer, and derived (not stored) statistics.

documentation/data-architecture/pki-sche.summary: Authoritative schema reference for the Project Knowledge Index (PKI) at {project_root}/.

documentation/data-architecture/progress.summary: Authoritative schema reference for worker progress files at dashboards/{id}/progress/{task_id}.

documentation/data-architecture/xml-task.summary: Schema reference for task files at tasks/{MM_DD_YY}/parallel_{name}.

documentation/multi-dashboard/archive-hi.summary: Documents Synapse's two complementary preservation systems for swarm data: archives (full directory snapshots in Archive/{YYYY-MM-DD}_{task_name}/) and history (lightweight JSON summaries in history/{YYYY-MM-DD}_{task_name}.

documentation/multi-dashboard/dashboard-.summary: Specifies how Synapse commands choose which dashboard to act on.

documentation/multi-dashboard/overview.m.summary: Introduces Synapse's multi-dashboard architecture: dashboards are dynamically created with 6-char hex IDs (no fixed slot limit), each fully isolated with its own initialization.

documentation/multi-dashboard/queue-syst.summary: Describes Synapse's overflow queue at {tracker_root}/queue/ that holds swarm plans when all active dashboards are occupied.

archive-then-clear-then-verify.pattern: Archive-then-clear-then-verify when reusing a pre-assigned dashboard with prior data: copy to Archive/{date}_{task_name}/, rm -f progress/*.json, reset logs.json, verify ls is empty before writing initialization.json.

convention-map-categories-naming-file.pattern: convention_map categories: naming, file_structure, imports, frontend_styling, backend_api, error_handling, testing, types — held in working memory, used to filter per-worker.

dashboard-merges-initialization-json-sta.pattern: Dashboard merges initialization.json (static) with progress/{task_id}.json files (dynamic) client-side — all stat counts derive from progress files.

repair-task-ids-r-suffix.pattern: Repair task IDs use 'r' suffix and don't break the regex: '2.1r' is valid.

6-step-procedure-parse-read.pattern: 6-step procedure: parse → read logs.json → apply filters in sequence → render table → handle empty → summary line

dispatch-sequence-launch-agent-first.pattern: Dispatch sequence: launch agent FIRST → log AFTER dispatch → write master_state checkpoint after batch
